# Contract: HDFC Reader FFI (UniFFI Swift boundary)

**Feature**: `003-hdfc-cc-parser` | **Date**: 2026-07-15
**Module**: `kaname-core` (Rust) → `KanameCore` (Swift, generated by UniFFI)

Additive to the P1 + ICICI `engine-ffi` contracts. The "external interface" is the exported
UniFFI surface (not an HTTP API); generated Swift signatures are the consumer-facing form.
**Reuses** the existing `Decimal`/`NaiveDate` custom types, the `Direction` enum, and the
`ParsedStatement`/`ParsedTransaction` records **unchanged** — HDFC adds **two functions and no
new type**.

---

## Exported functions

### `read_hdfc_statement`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn read_hdfc_statement(lines: Vec<String>, full_text: String) -> ParsedStatement` |
| Swift | `public func readHdfcStatement(lines: [String], fullText: String) -> ParsedStatement` |

**Contract**
- Parses **already-extracted** text `lines` (one statement row per element) + `full_text` (used
  only for enrichment). **Never** opens a file or PDF (FR-018).
- **Two layouts behind one call**: tries the **year-end** layout first, falls back to the
  **monthly** layout; returns the first layout that produced rows, else an empty (still enriched)
  statement — the caller never selects a layout (FR-003/004, SC-004). A statement matching neither
  layout returns empty `lines` with no error (US2-S4).
- Produces one `ParsedTransaction` per matching row; non-matching lines are ignored (FR-005/007);
  rows matching the shape but failing date/amount parse go to `ParsedStatement.errored_lines`
  (FR-017) — **never throws, never aborts**.
- **Direction from the statement, both layouts**: year-end from the `DR`/`CR` marker
  (`CR → .credit`, `DR → .debit`); monthly from a leading `+` (`+ → .credit`, else `.debit`) —
  never the amount's sign (FR-010/011/012).
- **Amount** is an exact non-negative `Decimal` with scale preserved; the monthly Rupee-glyph `C`
  is excluded from the number (FR-008/009, SC-006).
- Enriches `period_start` + `period_end` (year-end: first/last day of the opening/closing month;
  monthly: the Billing-Period dates) and `card_last4` (masked PAN via the `Card Number` anchor)
  from `full_text` when present; absent → left `nil` (FR-013/014/015/016).
- **Pure & deterministic**: identical input ⇒ identical output; no network, clock, locale, or
  global state (FR-019/022). Total function — no failure surfaced to Swift.

**Consumer expectations (app/test)**
- Call with PDFKit-extracted lines + full text. Treat `errored_lines` as review items; still
  consume every `lines` entry. Do not infer direction from `amount` — use `direction`. Do not
  assume a layout — the reader auto-selects.

### `hdfc_claims`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn hdfc_claims(full_text: String) -> bool` |
| Swift | `public func hdfcClaims(fullText: String) -> Bool` |

**Contract**
- Returns `true` iff `full_text` contains an HDFC credit-card issuer marker
  (`"HDFC Bank Credit Card"` or `"HDFC Bank Credit Cards"`, case-insensitive) — the
  document-plausibility gate (FR-001).
- MUST return `false` for other issuers' statements (e.g. an ICICI document) so a statement is
  never misattributed to HDFC (FR-002, US1-S4). Pure, deterministic, no I/O.

> Internally: `read_hdfc_statement` wraps
> `read_lines_first_match(&[&HdfcYearEndReader, &HdfcMonthlyReader], …)`; `hdfc_claims` delegates
> to the year-end config's `claims(&HdfcYearEndReader, …, "HDFC")` (both configs share the
> markers). `lib.rs` re-exports both functions (as it does the ICICI pair).

---

## Types (all reused — no new type)

- `record ParsedStatement` / `record ParsedTransaction` — **unchanged** from the ICICI slice
  (`contracts/../002-icici-cc-parser/contracts/engine-ffi.md`). HDFC now **populates
  `periodStart`** (ICICI left it `nil`).
- `enum Direction { Debit, Credit }` → Swift `.debit` / `.credit` (P1, `uniffi::Enum`).
- Custom types `Decimal`↔`String`↔`Foundation.Decimal` and `NaiveDate`↔ISO-8601 `String`
  (P1, in `ffi.rs` + `uniffi.toml`) — **unchanged**. Money never crosses as `f64`.

> Swift shape (for reference, unchanged): `ParsedStatement { bankCode: String; lines:
> [ParsedTransaction]; erroredLines: [String]; periodStart: String?; periodEnd: String?;
> cardLast4: String?; confidence: Double }`.

---

## Contract tests (test-first — FR-025/026/028)

**Rust (`cargo test`)** — engine-side truth (`tests/parity.rs` + unit tests):
- **Golden parity, year-end**: `read_hdfc_statement(year_end.lines, year_end.full_text)` equals
  the fixture's `expected` exactly — rows `2025-04-16 / 10610.00 / Credit / INR / "ONLINE TRF -
  PYMT RECD - THANK YOU"` and `2025-04-04 / 1071.00 / Debit / INR / "WWW EXAMPLE COM GURGAON"`;
  `period_start 2025-04-01`; `period_end 2026-03-31`; `card_last4 "9070"`; `errored_lines` empty.
- **Golden parity, monthly**: `read_hdfc_statement(monthly.lines, monthly.full_text)` equals the
  fixture's `expected` exactly — rows `2026-05-15 / 1639.00 / Debit / INR / "EXAMPLE MERCHANT
  BANGALORE"` and `2026-05-20 / 6738.00 / Credit / INR / "CC PAYMENT RECEIVED"`; `period_start
  2026-05-15`; `period_end 2026-06-14`; `card_last4 "5678"`; `errored_lines` empty. (Expected
  **captured from a live web-engine run** — never hand-derived.)
- **Determinism**: two calls on the same input are equal (both vectors).
- **Wrong issuer**: `hdfc_claims("ICICI Bank …") == false`; `hdfc_claims(year_end.full_text) == true`.
- **Malformed row**: a line matching an HDFC shape but with an unparseable date lands in
  `errored_lines`, valid rows still returned, no panic (reuses the existing behavior).

**Swift (Swift Testing, `import KanameCore`)** — "core ↔ Swift HDFC parse":
- Year-end: `readHdfcStatement(...)` over the two year-end lines → 2 `lines`; `lines[0]` =
  `2025-04-16`, `Decimal(string:"10610.00")`, `.credit`, `"INR"`; `lines[1]` = `2025-04-04`,
  `Decimal(string:"1071.00")`, `.debit`; `periodStart == "2025-04-01"`; `periodEnd ==
  "2026-03-31"`; `cardLast4 == "9070"`.
- Monthly: over the two monthly lines → `lines[0]` `.debit` `1639.00`, `lines[1]` `.credit`
  `6738.00`; `periodStart == "2026-05-15"`; `periodEnd == "2026-06-14"`; `cardLast4 == "5678"`.
- `hdfcClaims(fullText:) == true` for an HDFC text and `false` for an ICICI string.
- Amounts compared as `Foundation.Decimal` value-equality (exact; never float).

---

## Stability / compatibility
- Adding two functions is additive; reusing the records means **no wire-format change**.
- The generated `Generated/kaname_core.swift` + `KanameCoreFFI.xcframework` are build artifacts,
  rebuilt by `make core-xcframework` (which runs **before** `tuist generate`). **This contract**,
  not the generated files, is the reviewed source of truth.
